title: takt init: generated config.yaml missing allowed_tools_default and allowed_tools_by_agent
description:
generate_config_yaml() in onboarding.py produces a .takt/config.yaml that omits allowed_tools_default and allowed_tools_by_agent under the claude: block. When this config is loaded, BackendConfig.allowed_tools_default is an empty list, and allowed_tools_for() (config.py:79) returns [] — so the runner passes '--allowedTools ""' to claude. The agent has no tools whatsoever: no Write, no Edit, no Bash. It cannot implement anything.

Fix: update generate_config_yaml() in onboarding.py to include the full tool allowlist in the generated YAML, matching the built-in defaults in default_config() (config.py lines 128-144).

The generated claude: block should include:
  allowed_tools_default:
    - Edit
    - Write
    - Read
    - Bash
    - Glob
    - Grep
    - Skill
    - ToolSearch
    - WebSearch
    - WebFetch
  allowed_tools_by_agent:
    developer:
      - Agent
      - NotebookEdit
      - TaskCreate
      - TaskUpdate
      - TaskGet
      - TaskList
    tester:
      - Agent
      - TaskCreate
      - TaskUpdate
      - TaskGet
      - TaskList
    documentation:
      - NotebookEdit
    planner: []
    review: []

These values must stay in sync with the defaults in default_config(). Add a comment in generate_config_yaml() pointing at config.py default_config() so future changes to the defaults are not missed.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'takt init' on a fresh project and then 'takt run' produces agents that can write files
The generated .takt/config.yaml contains allowed_tools_default with at least Edit, Write, Read, Bash, Glob, Grep
The generated .takt/config.yaml contains allowed_tools_by_agent with developer including Agent and TaskCreate
A comment in generate_config_yaml() references config.py default_config() to flag sync risk
